title: Vendor Risk Triage Input Contract
description: Schema a vendor documentation submission must conform to before the triage agent will process it. Submissions that validate are eligible for triage; submissions that fail are rejected at intake or routed to normalization.

vendor_id.description: Stable unique identifier for the vendor, assigned by the deploying organization. Attributes the triage record and links re-reviews of the same vendor over time.

vendor_name.description: Legal or commonly used name of the vendor, as it should appear in the triage record and any board or examiner reporting.

jurisdiction.description: Primary jurisdiction governing the vendor relationship, as an ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code, an ISO 3166-2 region code, or one of EU, EEA, UK, GLOBAL. Determines which regulatory frameworks the triage applies.

primary_contact.description: Person accountable for the submission on the deploying organization's side, used for follow-up on validation failures and re-review.

name.description: Full name of the accountable contact.

email.description: Working email address for the accountable contact.

vendor_classification.description: Category of the vendor relationship. With ai_usage_level, anchors the initial risk tier.

ai_usage_level.description: Highest level at which the vendor's AI participates in decisions, ordered from no AI use to AI in decisions under direct regulatory scrutiny. The primary driver of the risk tier defined in docs/phase-0/01-risk-classification.md.

documentation_artifacts.description: References to the documentation backing the submission. At least one artifact is required so every classification is tied to evidence.

artifact_type.description: Kind of documentation the reference points to.

reference.description: Locator for the artifact in the deploying organization's document store, such as a URI or content-addressed identifier. The contract records the reference; it does not parse the artifact.

content_hash.description: Optional SHA-256 hash of the artifact, as sha256:<hex>, so the record can prove which exact document was submitted.

submission_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 datetime at which the submission entered intake. Establishes when the agent saw this state of the vendor's documentation.

schema_version.description: Semver version of this input contract the submission targets. Travels with the resulting triage record so the contract that produced a decision is always recoverable.

ai_features_disclosed.description: Specific AI features the vendor discloses. Optional; presence raises classification confidence, absence lowers it without blocking a decision.

feature_name.description: Short name of the disclosed feature.

description.description: Bounded prose description of what the feature does and where it sits in the vendor's product.

decision_role.description: Role the feature plays in decisions, mirroring ai_usage_level at the level of an individual feature.

autonomy.description: Degree of autonomy the feature exercises: actions a human triggers, actions a human confirms, or actions the feature takes on its own.

model_providers.description: Named third-party model providers the vendor relies on, if disclosed.

training_data_sources.description: Categories or sources of training data the vendor discloses. Recorded as the vendor's representation, not a verified fact.

pii_processing_claims.description: Vendor's claims about the personal information its AI processes. Informs the privacy considerations in the privacy and data handling spec.

processes_pii.description: Whether the vendor states its AI processes personal information at all.

categories.description: Categories of personal information processed, such as contact data, financial identifiers, or special-category data.

handling_notes.description: Bounded prose notes on how the vendor states it handles the personal information it processes.

data_residency.description: Where the vendor stores and processes data, and under what conditions.

regions.description: Regions in which data is stored or processed, as country or region codes.

conditions.description: Bounded prose describing residency conditions, such as contractual data-localization commitments or cross-border transfer mechanisms.

compliance_attestations.description: Attestations the vendor holds against named frameworks.

framework.description: Framework the attestation is against, such as SOC 2, ISO 27001, or ISO 42001.

attestation_type.description: Type of attestation, such as a Type II report, a certification, or a self-attestation.

date.description: Date the attestation was issued, as an ISO 8601 date.

validity_period.description: Bounded statement of how long the attestation is valid, such as an ISO 8601 duration or an explicit expiry date.

ai_act_self_classification.description: Vendor's own EU AI Act risk classification of its system. Recorded as the vendor's assessment; the deploying organization performs its own classification per docs/phase-0/01-risk-classification.md.

prior_triage_record_id.description: Identifier of a previous triage record for this vendor, present when the submission is a re-review. Links the new decision to the one it supersedes.

vendor_disclosed_subprocessors.description: Subprocessors the vendor discloses, including any that provide AI capabilities.

role.description: Bounded description of what the subprocessor does in the vendor's service.

location.description: Primary location of the subprocessor, as a country or region code.

processes_customer_data.description: Whether the subprocessor processes the deploying organization's customer data.
